title: lappa vulgaris var. minor
intro: Lappa vulgaris var. minor, also known as the lesser knapweed or small knapweed, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family Asteraceae. It is commonly found in dry meadows and fields in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Lappa vulgaris var. minor include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive problems, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health.
